steps.title: Look for a program overview
steps.content: The student wants an overview of the National Wildlife Refuge program, so the response should use information that applies broadly to the program. The notes give three broad features: there are more than 500 NWRs, they exist across the United States, and the listed examples show refuges protecting endangered species on both coasts.

steps.title: Reject narrow refuge details
steps.content:
• Choice B discusses only the Crocodile Lake NWR and the Nestucca Bay NWR, so it is narrower than a program overview.
  • Choice C gives one detail about Oregon's Nestucca Bay NWR and the Aleutian Canada goose.
  • Choice D centers on the Crocodile Lake NWR's acreage in Florida.
The remaining option combines the program's size, national reach, and endangered-species purpose. The answer is A.
